5-meo-xxt.note: Some of the 5-MeO tryptamines are a bit unpredictable and should be mixed with MDMA with care

alcohol.note: The combination of MDMA and alcohol may increase strain on the cardiovascular system and lead to increased alcohol consumption. MDMA may also impair one's ability to recognize their level of drunkenness, leading to increased alcohol consumption and poor decision-making. Alcohol may also increase the risk of dehydration and hyperthermia (increased body temperature) when combined with MDMA.

sources.title: Interactions between ethanol and cocaine, amphetamine, or MDMA in the rat: thermoregulatory and locomotor effects. (2008)

sources.title: Acute Effects of 3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) on Behavioral Measures of Impulsivity: Alone and in Combination with Alcohol. (2006)

sources.author: Hernández-López, C., Farré, M., Roset, P. N., Menoyo, E., Pizarro, N., Ortuño, J., Torrens, M., Camı́, J., & Torre, R. de la.
sources.title: 3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (Ecstasy) and Alcohol Interactions in Humans: Psychomotor Performance, Subjective Effects, and Pharmacokinetics. (2002)

sources.author: van Amsterdam, J., Brunt, T. M., Pierce, M., & van den Brink, W.
sources.title: Hard Boiled: Alcohol Use as a Risk Factor for MDMA-Induced Hyperthermia: a Systematic Review. (2021)

amphetamines.note: This combination of stimulants will increase strain on the heart, may cause some physical discomfort, and has the chance to cause cardiovascular issues. The anxiogenic and focusing effects of stimulants can increase the chance of unpleasant thought loops and make the experience more uncomfortable, this combination raises these chances. Amphetamines will increase the neurotoxic effects of MDMA, in addition to causing further concerns of hyperthermia due to the inherent nature of the combination. It will also raise one's body temperature, also likely making the combination more neurotoxic.

sources.author: Kelly J. Clemens, Iain S. McGregor, Glenn E. Hunt, Jennifer L. Cornish
sources.title: MDMA, methamphetamine and their combination: possible lessons for party drug users from recent preclinical research

sources.author: Kelly J. Clemens, Iain S. McGregor, Glenn E. Hunt, Jennifer L. Cornish
sources.title: Repeated weekly exposure to MDMA, methamphetamine or their combination: long-term behavioural and neurochemical effects in rats

sources.author: P. Leon Brown, Eugene A. Kiyatkin
sources.title: Brain hyperthermia induced by MDMA (‘ecstasy’): modulation by environmental conditions

caffeine.note: Caffeine is not really necessary with MDMA and increases any neurotoxic effects from MDMA

cannabis.note: Large amounts of cannabis may cause strong and somewhat unpredictable experiences in combination with MDMA. Cannabis should be saved for towards the end of the experience if possible.

cocaine.note: Cocaine blocks some of the desirable effects of MDMA while increasing the risk of heart attack.

dox.note: The combined stimulating effects of the two can be uncomfortable. Coming down on the MDMA while the DOx is still active can be quite anxiogenic.

ghb/gbl.note: Large amounts of GHB/GBL may overwhelm the effects of MDMA on the comedown.

ketamine.note: No unexpected interactions, though likely to increase blood pressure but not an issue with sensible doses. Moving around on high doses of  this combination may be ill advised due to risk of physical injury.

sources.author: Lokesh Shahani
sources.title: Venlafaxine Augmentation With Lithium Leading to Serotonin Syndrome

sources.author: Charles DeBattista, Mehmet Sofuoglu, and Alan F. Schatzberg
sources.title: Serotonergic Synergism: The Risks and Benefits of Combining the Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors with Other Serotonergic Drugs

sources.author: Olivier Massot Ph.D, Jean-Claude Rousselle Ph.D, Marie-Paule Fillion MSc, Dominique Januel MD, Mathieu Plantefol MSc & Gilles Fillion Ph.D
sources.title: 5-HT1B Receptors: A Novel Target for Lithium: Possible Involvement in Mood Disorders

sources.author: Philip J. Cowen
sources.title: New drugs, old problems: Revisiting… Pharmacological management of treatment-resistant depression

maois.note: MAO-B inhibitors can increase the potency and duration of phenethylamines unpredictably. MAO-A inhibitors with MDMA will lead to hypertensive crises.

mephedrone.note: Risk of serotonin syndrome as both drugs raise serotonin levels. Stimulants also increase the neurotoxic effects of MDMA, in addition to concerns of high blood pressure and unnecessary strain on the heart. As well as potentially causing anxiety and greater physical discomfort due to the combination.

mxe.note: There have been reports of risky serotonergic interactions when the two are taken at the same time, but MXE taken to the end of an MDMA experience does not appear to cause the same issues.

pcp.note: This combination can easily lead to hypermanic states

pregabalin.note: There is a significant risk of seizures from this combination. Amphetamines increase respiration rate allowing for a higher dose of sedatives than normal. If amphetamines wears off first, recreational dosages of pregabalin may overcome the individual and cause respiratory arrest. Both substances at recreational dosages cause strain of the heart and may lead to further dangerous heart complications. Medical dosages are not reported to be a concern

tramadol.note: Tramadol and stimulants both increase the risk of seizures.

mdma.dose_note: NOTE: Higher dosages may increase neurotoxic effects
mdma.name: mdma

properties.detection: 1-3 days single use, 3-5 days heavy use
properties.dose: Oral Light: 40-75mg  Common: 75-125mg  Strong: 125-175mg  Heavy: 175mg+ | Insufflated/Rectal Light: 30-70mg  Common: 70-120mg Strong: 120-165mg  Heavy: 165mg+ | NOTE: Higher doses may increase neurotoxic effects
properties.general-advice: It is recommended to wait at minimu multiple months between MDMA rolls.
properties.summary: The drug has been associated with cognitive decline when taken in excess. The drug is used in a variety of ways, such as parties, music festivals, psychedelic therapy, and more. The drug often sold as powder or in pressed pills, and may be adulterated with other similar chemicals.
